steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a cross-text relationship question. The underlined claim in Text 1 says The Nutcracker should no longer be produced. The skill is identifying how Text 2's author would respond. The decisive clue is that Text 2 concedes the show is outdated but argues it should continue — and offers Hot Chocolate Nutcracker as proof that creative updates work.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• ‘By questioning the idea that the story of The Nutcracker is stuck in the past and by rejecting the suggestion that contemporary audiences would enjoy an updated version’: Text 2 actually agrees the show is outdated (“The Nutcracker is outdated”), so Text 2's author does not question that claim. The author also clearly supports updated versions, citing Allen's success. ✗
  • ‘By agreeing that contemporary audiences have largely stopped going to see performances of The Nutcracker because it's so old-fashioned’: Text 2 says the show is a holiday favorite that generates substantial income — the opposite of audiences having stopped attending. ✗
  • ‘By pointing out that most dance companies could increase their incomes by offering modernized versions of The Nutcracker’: Text 2 never claims modernized versions raise income for most companies; income is mentioned as a reason to keep the original, not as a benefit of updating. ✗
  • ‘By suggesting that dance companies should consider offering revised versions of The Nutcracker instead of completely rejecting the show’: Text 2 directly argues the show should be kept and updated (“there are creative ways to update the show”), countering Text 1's call for complete rejection. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
